fields.summary: How to set up first Jenkins pipeline for new Node.js microservice
fields.description: Our team is new to Jenkins and we need to set up a CI/CD pipeline for our Node.js microservice notification-service. We need to understand how to create a Jenkinsfile, how to connect our GitHub repository, how to run our Jest tests, build a Docker image, and push it to our container registry. We have been given access to the Jenkins instance but we do not know where to start.

content.text: We have Node 18, npm, and Docker available on our dev machines. Our GitHub repo is already created at company/notification-service. We have been told we need to use the shared Jenkins agent pool but we are not sure how to specify this in our Jenkinsfile.

Hi Meera, here are the steps. Create a Jenkinsfile in your repo root with agent label nodejs-agent for the shared pool. Add stages for Install (npm ci), Test (npm test), Build Docker image, and Push to registry. For GitHub connection: in Jenkins go to New Item, select Multibranch Pipeline, add your GitHub repo URL, and use the github-credentials credential ID already configured. For Docker registry access use the registry-credentials credential ID. I will share a template Jenkinsfile for Node.js projects.

Pipeline is working now. First build passed all stages including Docker push. The template Jenkinsfile was very helpful. One suggestion - this information should be in the developer portal as a getting started guide. We spent 2 days trying to figure this out before raising this ticket.
